texto: Have you failed today? If not, you might not be trying hard enough. Failure is a part of growth, a stepping stone towards success. In its own right it is a small victory and should be celebrated as such. So I ask you again, have you failed today?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Shane E. Bryan que provoca esforço, persistência e resultados. Ao questionar “Have you failed today? If not, you might not be trying hard enough. Failure is a part of growth, …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre motivacao, failure, inspiracional, com foco em inspiracao e inspiracional.

frase_original: Have you failed today? If not, you might not be trying hard enough. Failure is a part of growth, a stepping stone towards success. In its own right it is a small victory and should be celebrated as such. So I ask you again, have you failed today?

texto: I do not have a disability, I have a gift! Others may see it as a disability, but I see it as a challenge. This challenge is a gift because I have to become stronger to get around it, and smarter to figure out how to use it; others should be so lucky.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Shane E. Bryan sobre temas ligados a motivacao. A afirmação “I do not have a disability, I have a gift! Others may see it as a disability, but I see it as a c…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a motivacao, disability, inspirational-attitude, útil em momentos de inspiracao e inspirational-attitude.
